title: Ollama and MetaTrader 5 Software
author: Just_Technology_8815
selftext:
I built a software platform that gives Ollama models, such as `gemma4:e4b`, direct access to MetaTrader 5 for AI-assisted trading workflows. MetaTrader 5 supports automated trading and external bot-style integrations, which makes it a practical base for this kind of system, while trading dashboards are commonly designed around unified visibility, monitoring, and trade analysis.  
The project is currently still in its testing phase, but several core modules are already integrated and working together as part of a broader local AI trading.

# Expanded version

I developed a trading software system that connects local Ollama models, for example `gemma4:e4b`, with MetaTrader 5 so the model can participate in market analysis and trading decisions inside a controlled workflow. MetaTrader 5 is widely used for algorithmic trading because it supports automated strategies, quote analysis, testing, optimization, and trading robot.  


At the center of the platform is a dashboard that gives a structured overview of everything at once. The interface is designed to be clear and grid-based, so key information can be monitored quickly without jumping between multiple windows, which aligns with how modern trading dashboards are often built around one unified.   


https://preview.redd.it/xmjxssa3zzug1.png?width=1617&format=png&auto=webp&s=5d4653af4305f420ead3e879b7a7b308473bb3d9

One of the more interesting components is the debate module. In this section, two prompt-driven AI models argue with each other about whether a position should be bought or sold, and after both sides present their reasoning, a separate “judge” model decides which conclusion appears more rational or actionable. This creates a multi-agent decision layer instead of relying on a single raw model.

https://preview.redd.it/0bli1kj9zzug1.png?width=1617&format=png&auto=webp&s=db8c908652368772cfc5278f0dfc73e3851a40bf

There is also an integrated trading journal, although it currently works only moderately well. The plan is to expand it so that all trades are automatically stored in Supabase, where they can later be filtered, reviewed, and analyzed in much more detail, similar to how trading journals are used to track performance and refine strategies over.

https://preview.redd.it/acd5atzbzzug1.png?width=1617&format=png&auto=webp&s=f89c38869dc9a12e99bd741c37c30d0a9401f9ca

Another important module is the economic calendar integration. The system takes scheduled macroeconomic events into account, and when a relevant event is approaching, such as US Core PPI, trading is automatically paused 30 minutes before the release and resumes 15 minutes afterward. This is meant to reduce unnecessary exposure during periods of abnormal volatility around major.

https://preview.redd.it/thuy0vnizzug1.png?width=1617&format=png&auto=webp&s=25d28f88834fbb7c44aa5375f07c7545132278a9

https://preview.redd.it/fx7qpk4kzzug1.png?width=1617&format=png&auto=webp&s=d59810e84087d0aae1d160e3279cdfb6c556cb24

  
In addition, the platform includes a GPU-based reinforcement learning area that is used to continuously “feed” or improve the local AI environment. The idea is to combine live market structure, model reasoning, and machine learning components so the system can evolve beyond static prompts and move toward adaptive behavior. AI-based trading setups are commonly described as benefiting from machine learning and continuous model-driven analysis, although risk still remains a.

https://preview.redd.it/e3mkneilzzug1.png?width=1617&format=png&auto=webp&s=8001383c4af1eb2dfe45fa50426e3bf273bd35e1

For technical monitoring, I also integrated a terminal view where warnings, logs, and errors can be seen directly. This makes debugging easier during development and testing, especially in a system where multiple components such as MetaTrader 5, local models, journal logic, and event controls interact in.

https://preview.redd.it/jofq9hgmzzug1.png?width=1617&format=png&auto=webp&s=5d6d60d2938d2302abd27b96c27e59da36b93270

Finally, there is a configuration tab where all important settings should be adjustable from one place. The goal is to make strategy behavior, trading rules, safety limits, and system-level options configurable without needing to manually edit code each time, which follows the general pattern of trading automation tools that expose risk, timing, and execution settings to the.

https://preview.redd.it/zaxpgwknzzug1.png?width=1617&format=png&auto=webp&s=8434d7e51aefa5360d2e7f9c97e0b42f465cf47d

  
**Conclusion**  
**This project represents a cutting-edge fusion of local AI, algorithmic trading, and robust risk management—pioneering autonomous trading systems that learn, debate, and adapt in real markets. Currently battle-tested with demo capital, it's poised for live deployment and continuous evolution through GPU reinforcement learning.**

comments.author: Paul_Kuhn
comments.body: And when you finished testing, what will you do? Sell it?

replies.author: Just_Technology_8815
replies.body:
No, I’m not going to sell it, I don’t see it spending money on something like that or needing a subscription. I may simply publish a “slightly” modified version with fewer features on github.

But what I do after that, I don’t know. This is not the Holy Grail, is it? ....

replies.author: Just_Technology_8815
replies.body: I think I will constantly optimize the program until the end of my life, I know that sounds stupid, a functioning system should not be changed, but the market is constantly changing. From that ;)

comments.author: Specific-King-5166
comments.body: The slippage is going to murder you before ollama even finishes generating the first token of "buy". unless you're running a dual h100 setup just to decide whether to long gold or not, you’re basically bringing a spoon to a nuclear knife fight. by the time your local llama-3-70b figures out the sentiment, the market has already moved 40 pips and the institutional bots have already cashed out and gone to lunch.

replies.author: Just_Technology_8815
replies.body:
That’s an interesting point. Actually, I “only” have an RTX 4080. Of course, it’s true that banks and financial institutions have bigger and more powerful machines. This project is intended solely for Forex trading. I wouldn’t trade gold, indices, or even crypto. And besides, I don’t want to trade in milliseconds, but rather in longer or different time frames.

I understand your approach, but this program is designed purely so that even a “normal mortal” can trade Forex.
